refnamediv.refpurpose: Create a new video file to write.

refsynopsisdiv.title: Syntax
refsynopsisdiv.synopsis: n = avifile(filename, dims) n = avifile(filename, dims, fps) n = avifile(filename, dims, fps, fourcc)

listitem.para: A string, the video filename to be created.
A 1x2 vector, which indicates the frame size (width, height).
Four-character codec code. Common AVI choices include "MJPG", "XVID", and "DIVX". Codec availability depends on the OpenCV video backend.
A number, the opened video file index.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: avifile creates a video writer through the OpenCV video I/O backend. The output directory must already exist and must be writable.
A relative filename is resolved from pwd. Use fullfile with an explicit destination such as TMPDIR when Scilab may have been launched from a protected directory.
"MJPG" is the recommended broadly compatible codec for AVI output. Other FOURCC values may be remapped by FFmpeg or may be unavailable in a particular OpenCV build.
After the video file is created, addframe can be used to add frame to the file. Remember to close the opened file using aviclose(n) or avicloseall().
Video support for IPCV is only available when IPCV is compiled with OpenCV which support video I/O.

refsection.title: Examples
